headline: Nvidia GTC Hype vs Stock Reaction: Why Isn't $NVDA Moving More?

He also analyzes Micron's HBM4 production and its strong gross margins, suggesting a durable pricing power in the memory market.

headline: The Week in Numbers: Temu fined, Micron's massive valuation
summary: STORY: From a big fine for Temu to Micron's massive valuation.:: Micron:: The Week In NumbersThis is The Week in Numbers.:: $232 million$232 million dollars is how much Chinese online retailer Temu has been fined by the European Union.EU tech regulators say the company failed to stop the sale of illegal products under the Digital Services Act.The Act requires large online companies to do more to tackle illegal and harmful content on their platforms.In a statement Temu says it respects the DSA objectives but disagrees with the Commission's decision. :: $1.43 billion$1.43 billion is the amount of damages the Australian government is seeking in its lawsuit against U.S. chemical maker 3M.That's after its firefighting foam it supplied contained so-called "forever chemicals'.It's the country's largest ever legal claim after the foam was used at 28 defense bases across the country. 3M said it would defend itself against the claims in court. :: $150 billion$150 billion a year is what Nvidia's chief executive said the chip company plans to invest in Taiwan.CEO Jensen Huang termed it the "epicenter" of the AI revolution and predicted it will be the world's tech manufacturing hub for a long time.His comments came at the launch celebration in Taipei for the chipmaker's planned Taiwan headquarters.:: $1 trillion:: Micron$1 trillion is the market value chipmaker Micron Technology topped for the first time.It crowns a dizzying rally that has cemented the largest U.S. memory chipmaker as one of the standout winners of the AI boom. Micron's shares rose more than 18% on Tuesday, pushing the stock to a record high.:: 8%:: Ferrari8% is the plunge Ferrari shares took on Tuesday after the auto icon unveiled it's first electric vehicle.The five-seat sedan bears little resemblance to Ferraris of the past, drawing a cool response from fans, and investors. CEO Benedetto Vigna defended the new model and said the company is receiving "strong interest" from new and existing customers.

retailer Temu has been fined by the European Union.EU tech regulators say the company failed to stop the sale of illegal products under the Digital Services Act.The Act requires large online companies to do more to tackle illegal and harmful content on their platforms.In a statement Temu says it respects the DSA objectives but disagrees with the Commission's decision. :: $1.43 billion$1.43 billion is the amount of damages the Australian government is seeking in its lawsuit against U.S. chemical maker 3M.That's after its firefighting foam it supplied contained so-called "forever chemicals'.It's the country's largest ever legal claim after the foam was used at 28 defense bases across the country. 3M said it would defend itself against the claims in court. :: $150 billion$150 billion a year is what Nvidia's chief executive said the chip company plans to invest in Taiwan.CEO Jensen Huang termed it the "epicenter" of the AI revolution and predicted it will be the world's tech manufacturing hub for a long time.His comments came at the launch celebration in Taipei for the chipmaker's planned Taiwan headquarters.:: $1 trillion:: Micron$1 trillion is the market value chipmaker Micron Technology topped for the first time.It crowns a dizzying rally that has cemented the largest U.S. memory chipmaker as one of the standout winners of the AI boom. Micron's shares rose more than 18% on Tuesday, pushing the stock to a record high.:: 8%:: Ferrari8% is the plunge Ferrari shares took on Tuesday after the auto icon unveiled it's first electric vehicle.The five-seat sedan bears little resemblance to Ferraris of the past, drawing a cool response from fans, and investors. CEO Benedetto Vigna defended the new model and said the company is receiving "strong interest" from new and existing customers.

headline: Samsung ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
summary: STORY: Samsung Electronics made a big move in the AI chip race on Friday.It said it's started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory - or HBM - chip to customers.It pulls the South Korean tech giant ahead of rivals as they compete to send out new versions of HBMs which are critical to AI data centers.The new chip is the 12-layer HBM4E and Samsung said it's more than 20% faster than its previous-generation HBM4 products.The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market.It had fallen behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced AI memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.Samsung's clients include major AI players Nvidia, AMD and Google, among others.Demand has surged for advanced memory chips used in AI servers and processors.Investors liked what they heard Friday.Shares of Samsung Electronics rose as much as 6.5% in morning trading.Rival SK Hynix also saw its shares climb just over 1%.

raw.headline: Samsung ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
raw.summary: STORY: Samsung Electronics made a big move in the AI chip race on Friday.It said it's started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory - or HBM - chip to customers.It pulls the South Korean tech giant ahead of rivals as they compete to send out new versions of HBMs which are critical to AI data centers.The new chip is the 12-layer HBM4E and Samsung said it's more than 20% faster than its previous-generation HBM4 products.The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market.It had fallen behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced AI memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.Samsung's clients include major AI players Nvidia, AMD and Google, among others.Demand has surged for advanced memory chips used in AI servers and processors.Investors liked what they heard Friday.Shares of Samsung Electronics rose as much as 6.5% in morning trading.Rival SK Hynix also saw its shares climb just over 1%.

headline: Key Themes to Watch at Asia’s Biggest AI Tech Show
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang leads a parade of AI computing leaders in Taiwan for Asia’s biggest technology showcase, Computex. Over a jam-packed week, the hardware linchpins of the post-ChatGPT era will debate the most pressing issues facing their industry, from growing bottlenecks in the supply of essential components like memory chips to the rise of challengers to Nvidia at the apex of the semiconductor hierarchy.Most Read from BloombergSingapore Hands Byju's Founder His First E

headline: Samsung Electronics ships faster HBM4E chip samples to customers; shares jump
summary: Samsung Electronics on Friday said it started shipping samples of its latest high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chip to customers, pulling ahead of rivals in distributing a new version of the product critical to ‌AI data centers and sending its shares higher.  Samsung said the chip uses its latest 1c DRAM process technology - sixth-generation, 10-nanometer-class DRAM - alongside Samsung's 4-nanometer foundry ​logic base die.  The rollout is part of Samsung's efforts to regain momentum in the HBM market after falling behind rivals like SK Hynix and Micron in supplying advanced artificial intelligence memory chips, particularly to Nvidia.
